model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=50 dy=160 conf=1.00 -> (411,365)
  iter 1: at (411,365) said dx=90 dy=-70 conf=0.80 -> (501,295)
  iter 2: at (456,330) said dx=-70 dy=-43 conf=1.00 -> (386,287)
  iter 3: at (438,319) said dx=-48 dy=-21 conf=0.90 -> (390,298)
  iter 4: at (426,314) said dx=-31 dy=-18 conf=0.90 -> (396,296)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the "CLICK ME" button.
The current crosshair position is at the center of the image, which is approximately (418, 309) based on the bottom right text.
Looking at the image:
- The "CLICK ME" button is a green rectangle.
- The red crosshair is currently positioned on the right side of the button.
- The center of the button is to the left of the red crosshair.
- The vertical position of the crosshair seems to be roughly centered on the button.

Let's estimate the distance from the crosshair to the center of the button:
- The button width is about 100 pixels.
- The crosshair is roughly at the right edge of the green area.
- The center of the button would be about 40-50 pixels to the left of the current crosshair.
- Vertically, the crosshair is very close to the center.

Let's re-examine.
The button text "CLICK ME" is to the left of the crosshair.
The green box extends from roughly x=450 to x=550 in screen coordinates? No, the coordinates are given at the bottom: (418, 309).
Wait, the crosshair is at (418, 309).
The button is a green rectangle.
The red crosshair is inside the green rectangle, towards the right side.
The center of the green rectangle is to the left.
Estimated offset: dx = -40, dy = 0.
Let's look closer at the image.
The red crosshair is exactly at the center of the blue circle.
The blue circle is centered on the right half of the green button.
The button's center is likely where the "I" or "C" in "CLICK" is.
The distance from the crosshair to the center of the button seems to be around 40 pixels to the left.
Vertical offset seems minimal, maybe 0.

Actually, looking at the button, the red crosshair is almost at the right edge of the green area.
The center of the green button is approximately 40-50 pixels to the left.
dx = -45, dy = 0. Confidence = 0.9.
